story.text: As a session, I need to OPEN an owner decision onto bdo's GitHub surface and REOPEN a wrongly-closed issue — both as recorded, attributed acts — so the owner-harness has a governed door for putting work in front of bdo and for reversing a stale auto-close, instead of a raw `gh` bypass (the prompt-parity hole) or a CLI line at bdo.

briefing.headline: The issue pen gains open + reopen — the recovery and owner-surface verbs
briefing.value: The issue pen could close and comment; it could not OPEN (so a session bypassed with raw gh or handed bdo a CLI line) or REOPEN (so a wrong/stale auto-close was a silent dead end). This adds both as governed verbs that record provenance on the log, the same accountability close already carries.
briefing.why_now: The #348 lesson: a non-dry 'dry run' auto-closed an owner directive; recovery needed a reopen verb, and close needed to be opt-in behind a non-empty guard. And the owner-harness needs an open verb so a decision reaches bdo's surface as a recorded act, not a CLI-at-owner fallback.
briefing.if_accepted: open/reopen are the paved paths for putting an owner decision on GitHub and for reversing a wrong close; both refuse before any gh call and record only after success.
briefing.if_rejected: Sessions keep bypassing with raw gh (the prompt-parity hole) and a wrong auto-close stays a dead end.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: additive verbs on an existing pen (126 insertions, 2 deletions); each refuses empties + missing signer before touching gh and writes a record only on success. Reverting is deleting two functions.
briefing.mechanism: do_open creates the issue (gh issue create), parses the number from the printed /issues/ URL, appends one issue.opened record; do_reopen runs gh issue reopen --comment <reason> (verified flag) and appends issue.reopened; both raise ValueError on empty/missing-signer BEFORE any gh call and append only when returncode==0; comment gains --body-file.

source_artifacts: registered as a piece of epic.owner-harness (the confirmed arc that backs it)
